title: Why Do Stars Blink?
blurb: Join little Wren and Professor Hoot as they discover the secret dance of the night sky.

spreads.spread_text: Look at the night sky.
spreads.scene_brief: Impressionist garden. Dabs of blue and deep purple paint create a soft evening glow.

spreads.spread_text: It is so very large.
spreads.scene_brief: Wren stands small against a massive sky. Vibrant brushstrokes of light surround her.

spreads.spread_text: What are those tiny lights?
spreads.scene_brief: Professor Hoot perches on a branch. His feathers catch the golden light.

spreads.spread_text: Wait! Did that star blink?
spreads.scene_brief: A bright white dot flickers. Bold, impasto paint highlights the movement.

spreads.spread_text: It blinked twice, Professor!
spreads.scene_brief: Wren points upward with a surprised face. The colors around her are lively.

spreads.spread_text: Let us find out why.
spreads.scene_brief: Professor Hoot adjusts his tiny glasses. He looks very wise in the moonlight.

spreads.spread_text: Is it a secret wink?
spreads.scene_brief: Wren imagines eyes in the stars. The painting is dreamy and hazy.

spreads.spread_text: Maybe they are sleepy stars.
spreads.scene_brief: Soft yellow strokes make the lights look fuzzy. Wren looks a bit worried.

spreads.spread_text: Do they have tiny eyes?
spreads.scene_brief: Hoot shakes his head. He looks thoughtful in the dappled light.

spreads.spread_text: They push up the steep grass together.
spreads.scene_brief: Wren and Hoot climbing a green hill at twilight.

spreads.spread_text: One more step! They both say.
spreads.scene_brief: The two friends looking upward with intent faces.

spreads.spread_text: The summit is almost within reach.
spreads.scene_brief: Wren reaching forward while Hoot encourages her.

spreads.spread_text: Together, the climb feels possible.
spreads.scene_brief: A wide shot of them on a high ridge.

spreads.spread_text: The night sky is so big and deep.
spreads.scene_brief: Looking at a massive, dark purple cosmos.

spreads.spread_text: Wren feels like a tiny speck.
spreads.scene_brief: Wren shrinking back into Hoot's feathers.

spreads.spread_text: The stars are much too far away.
spreads.scene_brief: Looking at distant tiny dots of light.

spreads.spread_text: Everything feels very large and cold.
spreads.scene_brief: Shadows stretching across the grass.

spreads.spread_text: Hoot points to the sparkling lights.
spreads.scene_brief: Hoot's wing gesturing toward a bright star.

spreads.spread_text: The air makes them shimmer and dance.
spreads.scene_brief: Twinkling light refracting through the atmosphere.

spreads.spread_text: Look how they spin in the breeze!
spreads.scene_brief: The stars appearing to swirl like jewels.

spreads.spread_text: A secret magic lives above us.
spreads.scene_brief: Wide-eyed wonder on Wren's face in the glow.

spreads.spread_text: They rest on the soft hill.
spreads.scene_brief: The two birds snuggled together under moonlight.

spreads.spread_text: Asking questions is where the adventure begins.
spreads.scene_brief: Wren looking brave with a small smile.

spreads.spread_text: Tomorrow brings brand new wonders.
spreads.scene_brief: A peaceful sleeping scene under the stars.
